q: What does 'delegation' mean for a canvas app data query?
o: The processing (filter/sort) is delegated to the data source instead of the device
The app copies all rows locally first
The user delegates the query to an admin
Power Fx runs the query twice for accuracy
why: Delegation pushes filtering, sorting and aggregation to the data source so only needed rows are returned.

q: Why is delegation important for apps over large data sources?
o: It avoids the client-side record limit by letting the source process all rows
It encrypts the data in transit
It lets you skip writing formulas
It converts the source to a collection
why: Without delegation, only the first N rows (the delegation limit) are processed locally, risking incomplete results.

q: Decide whether each statement about delegation is true.

rows.s: Whether a function is delegable depends on both the function and the data source.
The column type involved can also affect whether an operation is delegable.
Delegation support is identical across Dataverse, SharePoint and SQL.

why: Delegation depends on the function, the data source and sometimes the column type; support varies by connector.

q: You must choose a data source for an app that will grow to tens of thousands of rows. What should you weigh most?
o: Its delegation support for the functions you need
Its logo color
Whether it has an emoji picker
The number of screens in the app
why: Choosing a source with strong delegation support for your functions keeps large-data apps correct and fast.

q: A blue underline/warning appears under part of your Filter formula. What is Power Apps telling you?
o: That part isn't delegable and may not return all matching rows
The formula has a syntax error
The column is deprecated
The app exceeded its storage quota
why: A delegation warning flags a non-delegable operation that only processes rows up to the delegation limit.

q: When a query isn't delegable, how many records are processed locally by default, and what's the maximum you can set?
o: Default 500, up to a maximum of 2000
Default 100, up to 1000
Default 2000, unlimited
Default 50, up to 500
why: The non-delegable record limit defaults to 500 and can be raised to a maximum of 2000 in app settings.

q: Which is the SAFEST way to handle a genuinely non-delegable function you must use?
o: Restructure so delegable operations narrow the data first, or ensure results stay under the limit
Always raise the limit to 2000 and ignore the warning
Disable the data source
Move the formula to OnStart
why: Reduce the working set with delegable operations first; simply raising the limit still risks missing rows beyond 2000.

q: Decide whether each statement about delegation limits is true.

rows.s: A non-delegable function silently returns only the rows within the record limit.
Some functions are partially delegable depending on how they're used.
Raising the record limit guarantees you always get every matching row.

why: Non-delegable functions cap at the limit; some are partially supported; raising the cap to 2000 still isn't unlimited.

q: According to the module, what is the MOST common performance bottleneck in canvas apps?
o: The data sources and how the app calls them
The device battery
The number of labels on a screen
The app icon size
why: Data-source access patterns (excessive/inefficient calls) are the most common bottleneck.

q: Which are cited as data-source performance problems? (choose 2)
o: Too many refreshes of the data
Too many lookups against the source
Using too few screens
Naming variables in English
why: Excessive refreshes and repeated lookups are common performance drains; so is storing data in the wrong source.

q: Storing data in the 'wrong' data source hurts performance mainly because...
o: The source may not delegate the operations your app needs
It changes the app's theme
It disables Power Fx
It removes the gallery control
why: A poorly matched source can force non-delegable, slow client-side processing.

q: What is a recommended mindset for improving app performance?
o: Optimize in small, measurable steps
Rewrite the whole app at once
Ignore warnings until users complain
Add more data sources
why: The module advises optimizing performance incrementally in small steps.

q: How can a collection improve performance when the same data is read repeatedly?
o: Cache the data once in a collection and read from memory instead of re-querying
Delete the data source
Add a delegation warning
Convert it to a choice column
why: Caching stable data in a collection avoids repeated network round-trips.

q: When is caching data in a collection appropriate?
o: When the data is relatively stable during the session
When the data changes every second
Only for images
Never, collections are read-only
why: Cache data that won't change while in use; frequently changing data would go stale.

q: Decide whether each statement about collections and performance is true.

rows.s: Reading from an in-memory collection is generally faster than re-hitting the data source.
Cached collection data can become stale if the source changes.

why: Collections are fast local caches but do not auto-refresh from the source.

q: Which function loads a fresh set of records into a collection in a single call?
o: ClearCollect
Refresh
Notify
Navigate
why: ClearCollect empties and repopulates a collection in one step, ideal for (re)caching.

q: Which tool lets you watch your app's live network calls to diagnose performance?
o: Monitor
Solution Checker
SolutionPackager
The formula bar
why: Monitor traces events including network activity, revealing slow or excessive calls.

q: In Monitor, seeing many repeated calls to the same data source usually indicates...
o: An opportunity to cache or reduce redundant queries
That the app is working optimally
A licensing error
That delegation is disabled globally
why: Repeated identical calls signal redundant work you can cache or consolidate.

q: Decide whether each statement about diagnosing performance is true.

rows.s: Monitor can be used while the app runs to inspect each operation.
Network activity in Monitor helps spot excessive data-source calls.
You must publish the app before Monitor can show anything.

why: Monitor attaches to a running session (including in Studio) and surfaces network/events live.

q: After identifying a slow screen, what is a sound first optimization?
o: Reduce and cache the data the screen loads
Add more controls to the screen
Increase the record limit to 2000
Remove all formulas
why: Cutting and caching the data a screen requests attacks the most common bottleneck directly.

q: Which combination BEST keeps a large-data app both correct and responsive?
o: Delegable queries plus caching stable reference data
Raising the limit and caching everything forever
Disabling delegation and looping with ForAll
Loading all rows on OnStart
why: Delegation keeps results complete; caching stable data cuts redundant calls.

q: A gallery only shows 500 of thousands of rows. What's the root cause to investigate first?
o: A non-delegable operation in the gallery's Items
The gallery template color
A missing app icon
Too many screens
why: Hitting exactly the delegation limit points to a non-delegable Items formula.

q: Decide whether each performance guideline is true.

rows.s: Fewer, delegable data calls beat many client-side operations.
Concurrent loading can reduce total startup wait.

why: Delegable, consolidated and concurrent data access all improve responsiveness.

q: Which statement about the delegation limit is accurate?
o: It bounds how many rows non-delegable operations process locally
It limits how many screens an app can have
It caps the number of collections
It sets the app's frame rate
why: The limit (500 default, 2000 max) governs local processing of non-delegable queries only.
